codeReview.summary: The implementation fully covers all required authentication and role-based access control assertions (VAL-AUTH-001 through VAL-AUTH-006 and VAL-CROSS-001). The code is well-structured with clean separation of concerns: Accounts context handles domain logic, Auth plugs provide middleware protection, SessionController manages the sign-in/sign-out flows, and LiveView on_mount callbacks protect LiveView routes. The 36 tests comprehensively verify all contract assertions including positive flows (successful sign-in, admin access) and negative flows (invalid credentials, guest blocking, non-admin denial).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The phoenix-fullstack-worker skill was followed correctly: failing tests were written first (accounts_test.exs and auth_live_test.exs), then implementation was added until tests passed. The worker invoked elixir-expert skill before making code changes as required.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows worker invoked elixir-expert skill, then wrote tests first before implementing schemas, context, and UI components. handoff.skillFeedback.followedProcedure is true.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker correctly followed Phoenix 1.8 patterns: using Layouts.app with current_scope assign, verified_routes (~p sigil), live_session with on_mount callbacks for route protection, and to_form for form handling. The sign-in template correctly uses controller-based form (not LiveView) with standard POST action.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: All LiveView templates start with <Layouts.app flash={@flash} current_scope={@current_scope}>, router uses verified_routes, SessionController.new uses to_form/2 for controller-based form.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker properly handled Phoenix 1.8 auth patterns: the on_mount callbacks (:ensure_authenticated, :ensure_admin) correctly handle both atom and string keys for session token, which is a common pitfall when session keys can be serialized as either type.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: lib/predictions_web/plugs/auth.ex lines 103-104 and 123-124: user_token = session[:user_token] || session["user_token"]

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker correctly placed authentication plugs in the browser pipeline (fetch_current_user) rather than requiring manual plug calls per route. This follows Phoenix convention for authentication.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: lib/predictions_web/router.ex line 12: plug :fetch_current_user in browser pipeline

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker used Bcrypt.no_user_verify/0 to prevent timing attacks on non-existent users - a security best practice that should be documented as a pattern for future auth-related work.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: lib/predictions/accounts/user.ex line 57: Bcrypt.no_user_verify() in valid_password?/2, and lib/predictions/accounts.ex line 67: Bcrypt.no_user_verify() in authenticate_user/2

sharedStateObservations.observation: The login_user/1 helper in test/support/conn_case.ex provides a pattern for simulating authenticated users in tests by creating a session token and setting it in the test session. This pattern should be reused for future features requiring authenticated test scenarios.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: test/support/conn_case.ex lines 47-52: login_user/1 creates session token and initializes test session with user_token

summary: PASS - Complete and correct implementation of authentication and role-based access control. All 7 contract assertions (VAL-AUTH-001 through VAL-AUTH-006 and VAL-CROSS-001) are fully covered by the 36 tests. The code follows Phoenix 1.8 conventions, uses proper security practices (timing attack prevention, session renewal on login), and provides clean patterns for future features to build upon. No bugs or missing functionality detected.
